text: What is the primary objective of data persistence in web development?

options.text: To allow websites to have 'permanent memory' and save data across refreshes.
To increase the speed of the user's internet connection.
To convert JavaScript code into HTML automatically.
To hide the source code of the website from the browser.

text: Which format is considered the 'Universal Language of Data' for internet communication?

options.text: HTML
JSON
LocalDB
Python

text: In the shipping crate analogy, which function represents 'packing' your data into text?

options.text: JSON.parse()
JSON.stringify()
localStorage.setItem()
fetch()

text: What is the purpose of JSON.parse()?

options.text: To convert a text string back into a JavaScript object.
To delete data from the browser's memory.
To send a request to a server.
To create a visual table in the Application tab.

text: Where is LocalStorage data physically stored?

options.text: On a remote cloud server.
Directly in the user's web browser.
In the computer's RAM (cleared on shutdown).
Within the website's CSS files.

text: Which method is used to save a piece of data to LocalStorage?

options.text: localStorage.getItem()
localStorage.saveData()
localStorage.setItem()
localStorage.push()

text: To view saved LocalStorage data in Chrome, which Developer Tools tab should you use?

options.text: Console
Elements
Application
Network

text: What is the primary role of the Fetch API?

options.text: To style the user interface.
To make network requests and retrieve live data from the internet.
To encrypt the browser's history.
To speed up the JSON.parse() function.

text: What does the 'async' keyword do when placed before a function?

options.text: It ensures the function always returns a Promise.
It makes the function run instantly without waiting.
It prevents the function from accessing LocalStorage.
It converts the function's output into a JSON string.

text: Where can the 'await' keyword be used?

options.text: Anywhere in a JavaScript file.
Only inside an async function.
Only within a JSON object.
Inside the Application tab of Developer Tools.

text: What happens to the execution of an async function when it hits an 'await' keyword?

options.text: It pauses until the Promise is resolved.
It crashes the browser's main thread.
It skips the current line and moves to the next one.
It resets all variables in the function to null.

text: Why is Async/Await preferred over older Promise-chain (.then) syntax?

options.text: It makes the code look and behave more like synchronous, linear code.
It is the only way to use the Fetch API.
It automatically saves all data to LocalStorage.
It forces the user to refresh the page.

text: Which block is recommended for error handling when using Async/Await?

options.text: if...else
try...catch
wait...stop
JSON.error()

text: What is an API (Application Programming Interface) in the context of fetching data?

options.text: A special data-only website or service used to request live information.
A type of browser plugin that stores passwords.
A programming language used only for CSS.
The physical cable connecting a computer to the internet.

text: What does 'non-blocking' mean in the context of Async/Await?

options.text: The entire program freezes until the data arrives.
The user interface remains responsive while waiting for slow operations.
It blocks other websites from seeing your data.
It stops the internet connection to save power.

text: If you want to retrieve a saved value named 'username' from LocalStorage, which code is correct?

options.text: localStorage.getItem('username');
localStorage.putItem('username');
JSON.parse('username');
fetch('username');

text: When sending data over the internet, why can't we send JavaScript objects directly?

options.text: Computers must send plain text over the internet.
JavaScript objects are too heavy for cables.
Sending objects is illegal in most countries.
JavaScript objects only work on Apple computers.

text: In a standard fetch request using async/await, what is usually the first thing you 'await'?

options.text: The JSON.parse() function.
The fetch() call to the URL.
The localStorage.setItem() method.
The user clicking the refresh button.

text: What happens to data in LocalStorage when a user closes their browser tab?

options.text: It is immediately deleted.
It remains stored and is available when they return.
It is sent to the developer via email.
It is converted into an HTML file.

text: What is the result of the code: typeof JSON.stringify({age: 25})?

options.text: object
string
number
undefined
